title: Memory
description: Two-tier memory system: STM for session-scoped conversation state managed by AgentCore, and LTM for cross-session semantic recall stored in Aurora pgvector. Memory enables personalization, context continuity, and preference learning.
codeSnippet:
# STM — session-scoped via AgentCore Memory
await memory.store(session_id, turn_context)

# LTM — pgvector semantic recall
SELECT content, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) AS similarity
FROM memory_items
WHERE persona_id = $2 AND tier = 'ltm'
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1 LIMIT 5;

title: MCP
description: AgentCore MCP Gateway provides a standardized protocol for tool discovery and invocation. Tools are registered with semantic embeddings, enabling the orchestrator to discover relevant tools via natural-language queries rather than hardcoded routing.
codeSnippet:
# MCP tool discovery via AgentCore Gateway
gateway = MCPGateway(endpoint=settings.MCP_ENDPOINT)
tools = await gateway.discover(
    query="find products matching user intent",
    limit=5
)

title: State Management
description: Centralized state management coordinates conversation context, agent activation status, and routing decisions across the multi-agent system. AgentCore Runtime maintains the state graph that the orchestrator traverses.
codeSnippet:
# State transitions managed by AgentCore Runtime
state = {
    'intent': classified_intent,
    'active_agents': [search, recommend],
    'memory_context': stm_items,
    'routing': 'dispatcher'
}

title: Tool Registry / Gateway
description: Nine tools registered in Aurora PostgreSQL with pgvector embeddings. The gateway enables semantic discovery — agents find tools by describing what they need, not by knowing function names. HNSW indexing provides sub-millisecond lookup.

title: Skills
description: Two skills — style-advisor and gift-concierge — are injected at runtime based on persona context. The SkillRouter (Claude Haiku 4.5) evaluates each turn and activates the appropriate skill when the conversation signals a styling question or gift occasion.
codeSnippet:
# SkillRouter evaluates each turn
skill_router = Agent(
    model=BedrockModel('claude-haiku-4-5'),
    system_prompt=SKILL_ROUTER_PROMPT
)

# Activated skills inject into agent context
if skill == 'style-advisor':
    agent.tools.append(style_advisor_tool)

title: Runtime
description: AgentCore Runtime provides the execution environment for all agents — model invocation, tool orchestration, memory access, and observability hooks. It manages the lifecycle from intent classification through response composition.
codeSnippet:
# AgentCore Runtime initialization
from bedrock_agentcore import Runtime

runtime = Runtime(
    agents=[search, recommend, pricing],
    memory=memory_service,
    gateway=mcp_gateway,
    telemetry=otel_exporter
)

title: Evaluations
description: Evaluation scorecards measure agent accuracy, latency percentiles, and citation rates. Version-over-version trends track quality improvements as agents are refined. Each evaluation recipe tests a specific capability.

title: Grounding
description: All agent responses are grounded in data from Aurora PostgreSQL — product catalog, pricing, inventory, and return policies. pgvector embeddings enable semantic grounding, ensuring recommendations are factually anchored rather than hallucinated.
codeSnippet:
# Grounding query — verify product exists
SELECT id, name, price, in_stock
FROM product_catalog
WHERE id = $1;

# Semantic grounding — find related facts
SELECT content FROM knowledge_base
WHERE embedding <=> $1 < 0.3;
